title: Fridge Compartment Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The refrigerator compartment temperature has risen above the safe threshold.
fullDescription: Alarm 01 indicates the fridge compartment temperature exceeds the maximum safe level, typically above 9C (48F). Food safety may be compromised.

causes.cause: Large amount of warm food just loaded

fixSteps.title: Check the door
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the refrigerator door is fully closed. Check the gasket for damage or debris preventing a seal.

fixSteps.title: Reduce load temperature
fixSteps.instruction: If you just loaded warm food, allow time for the fridge to cool down. Avoid loading large quantities of hot food.

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser coils
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the fridge out and clean the condenser coils at the back or bottom with a brush or vacuum.

fixSteps.title: Check internal fan
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the internal circulation fan. If it is not running, the fan motor may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the compressor or fan has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Liebherr refrigerator alarm 01 temperature high fix

faq.q: Is my food safe with Al 01?
faq.a: If the temperature has been above 5C for more than 2 hours, perishable food may not be safe.

faq.q: How long does it take to cool down?
faq.a: After closing the door, it typically takes 2-4 hours to return to normal temperature.

faq.q: Why does the alarm keep beeping?
faq.a: The alarm continues until the temperature drops below the threshold. Press the alarm button to silence it temporarily.

title: Freezer Compartment Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The freezer compartment temperature has risen above the safe threshold.
fullDescription: Alarm 02 indicates the freezer temperature has exceeded the maximum safe level, typically above -12C (10F). Frozen food quality may be affected.

fixSteps.title: Check the door
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the freezer door is fully closed and the gasket seals properly.

fixSteps.title: Check for ice buildup
fixSteps.instruction: Look for frost or ice blocking the air vents inside the freezer. If present, a manual defrost may be needed.

fixSteps.title: Verify defrost system
fixSteps.instruction: If ice builds up frequently, the defrost heater, timer, or thermostat may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Check compressor operation
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the compressor running. If it is silent, the compressor or start relay may have failed.

whenToCallPro: If the defrost system or compressor needs repair.
videoSearchQuery: Liebherr freezer alarm 02 temperature high fix

faq.q: Should I throw away thawed food?
faq.a: If food has thawed and been above -4C for more than 2 hours, it may not be safe to refreeze.
Turn off the freezer, remove all food, leave the door open, and let ice melt naturally. Takes 4-8 hours.
Yes, overloading blocks air circulation. Ensure vents inside are not blocked by food.

title: BioFresh Compartment Temperature Error
shortDescription: The BioFresh compartment temperature is outside the target range.
fullDescription: Alarm 03 is specific to Liebherr models with BioFresh technology. The BioFresh compartment is not maintaining its near-zero degree temperature.

causes.cause: Temperature sensor in BioFresh compartment faulty

fixSteps.title: Check drawer closure
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the BioFresh drawers are fully closed and seated properly.

fixSteps.title: Adjust the vent slider
fixSteps.instruction: Check the humidity slider on the BioFresh drawers. Adjust to the correct setting for the type of food stored.

fixSteps.title: Verify BioFresh fan
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the BioFresh circulation fan. If not running, it may need replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check the BioFresh sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the alarm persists with correct settings, the temperature sensor in the BioFresh zone may need testing.

whenToCallPro: If the fan or sensor needs replacement.

faq.a: BioFresh is Liebherr's technology that maintains near-zero temperatures with controlled humidity for longer food freshness.
BioFresh operates close to 0C for optimal food preservation.
Keep the drawers clean and check the humidity slider settings regularly.

title: Door Open Alarm
shortDescription: A door has been left open for longer than the alarm threshold time.
fullDescription: Alarm 04 activates when any door has been left open for more than the configured time (typically 1-2 minutes). This prevents temperature rise and energy waste.

causes.cause: Items inside preventing door from closing

fixSteps.title: Close the door
fixSteps.instruction: Close the door firmly. If it bounces back, check for items inside blocking closure.

fixSteps.title: Rearrange items
fixSteps.instruction: Move any items that stick out past the shelf edge and prevent proper door closure.

fixSteps.title: Check the door gasket
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door seal for tears, gaps, or debris. Clean with warm soapy water.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: If the alarm triggers even when the door is closed, the door switch may be faulty.

whenToCallPro: If the door switch is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Liebherr refrigerator door open alarm fix gasket

faq.q: How long before the door alarm sounds?
faq.a: Typically 1-2 minutes after the door has been left open. Some models allow you to adjust this time.

faq.q: Can I disable the door alarm?
faq.a: On some models, yes, but it is recommended to keep it active for food safety.

faq.q: Why does my door not close properly?
faq.a: A worn gasket, uneven floor, or overloaded door shelves can prevent proper closure.

title: SuperCool Active Notification
shortDescription: The SuperCool rapid cooling function is active.
fullDescription: Alarm 05 is a notification (not an error) that the SuperCool function is running, which lowers the fridge temperature to rapidly cool newly added food.

causes.cause: Automatic SuperCool triggered by door opening pattern
Control board fault keeping SuperCool on

symptoms: Al 05 or SuperCool icon on display
Fridge temperature lower than set point
Ice crystals on some food items

fixSteps.title: Deactivate SuperCool
fixSteps.instruction: Press the SuperCool button on the control panel to manually deactivate it.

fixSteps.title: Wait for auto-off
fixSteps.instruction: SuperCool automatically deactivates after 6-12 hours depending on the model.

fixSteps.title: Check temperature setting
fixSteps.instruction: After SuperCool deactivates, verify the fridge temperature returns to the set point (recommended 5C).

fixSteps.title: Reset if stuck
fixSteps.instruction: If SuperCool does not deactivate, unplug for 5 minutes to reset the control board.

whenToCallPro: Only if SuperCool is stuck on and reset does not help.

faq.a: No, it is a feature that rapidly cools the fridge to protect food you just loaded.
Yes, temporarily. It increases compressor running time until it automatically deactivates.
Before loading a large amount of food, especially after shopping, to cool it quickly.

title: SuperFrost Active Notification
shortDescription: The SuperFrost rapid freezing function is active.
fullDescription: Alarm 06 indicates the SuperFrost function is running, which lowers the freezer to its minimum temperature for rapid freezing of fresh food.
symptoms: Al 06 or SuperFrost icon on display
Freezer temperature much lower than normal
Items in freezer extra hard frozen

fixSteps.title: Deactivate SuperFrost
fixSteps.instruction: Press the SuperFrost button to manually turn it off.

fixSteps.title: Wait for auto-off
fixSteps.instruction: SuperFrost automatically turns off after 24-72 hours depending on the model.

fixSteps.title: Check temperature
fixSteps.instruction: After deactivation, verify the freezer returns to the set point (recommended -18C).

fixSteps.title: Reset if stuck
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes if SuperFrost is stuck on.

whenToCallPro: Only if stuck on after reset.

faq.a: Activate SuperFrost 24 hours before adding a large batch of fresh food to the freezer.

faq.q: Does SuperFrost affect existing frozen food?
faq.a: The extra-cold temperature is beneficial for existing food and helps new food freeze faster.
For small additions, no. For large quantities of fresh food, SuperFrost preserves quality.

title: Fridge Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The refrigerator compartment temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Error E15 indicates the NTC thermistor sensor in the fridge compartment is open, shorted, or reading out of range.

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes to reset the control board.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the sensor inside the fridge compartment (usually near the top). Check the wire is not pinched.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure with a multimeter. At 25C expect approximately 10 kOhms.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install a matching Liebherr NTC sensor. Route the wire carefully.

whenToCallPro: If the control board is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Liebherr refrigerator E15 temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Can a faulty sensor cause food to freeze?
faq.a: Yes, if the sensor reads too warm, the compressor runs nonstop and freezes the fridge contents.
Usually mounted on the back wall near the top of the fridge compartment.
No, NTC sensors are very affordable at $10-$30.

title: Freezer Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The freezer compartment temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Error E16 indicates the NTC thermistor in the freezer is faulty, preventing proper temperature regulation.

causes.cause: Sensor damaged by ice during defrost

symptoms: Freezer too cold or not cold enough

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the freezer sensor, usually near the evaporator. Check for ice damage or disconnection.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: At room temp expect about 10 kOhms. At -18C the reading will be much higher.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install a matching Liebherr sensor in the same location.

whenToCallPro: If the control board is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Liebherr freezer E16 temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Does E16 affect the fridge compartment?
faq.a: E16 is specific to the freezer sensor. The fridge may continue working normally.
Yes, during defrost cycles, ice formation and melting can damage the sensor over time.
Address promptly to avoid food safety issues in the freezer.

title: Evaporator Fan Motor Failure
shortDescription: The evaporator fan motor in the freezer has stopped working.
fullDescription: Error E20 indicates the evaporator fan that circulates cold air through the freezer and fridge compartments has failed. Temperature distribution will be uneven.

causes.cause: Ice buildup blocking the fan blade

symptoms: Freezer warm at top but cold at bottom
No air circulation sound from freezer

fixSteps.title: Check for ice buildup
fixSteps.instruction: Open the freezer and look behind the back panel for ice blocking the fan. If present, defrost manually.

fixSteps.title: Listen for the fan
fixSteps.instruction: Close the door and listen. If the fan is not running, the motor may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Check fan rotation
fixSteps.instruction: With power off, try to spin the fan blade by hand. If it does not rotate freely, ice or bad bearings.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is confirmed faulty, remove the back panel inside the freezer, disconnect the old motor, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the motor needs replacement and you are not comfortable with the repair.
videoSearchQuery: Liebherr refrigerator E20 evaporator fan motor replacement

faq.a: Yes, ice buildup from a defrost issue can block the fan blade and prevent rotation.

faq.q: Why is my fridge warm but freezer cold?
faq.a: The evaporator fan distributes cold air from the freezer to the fridge. If the fan fails, the fridge gets warm.

faq.q: How long does the fan motor last?
faq.a: Typically 8-15 years with normal use.

title: Electronic Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board has a communication fault with the display or sensor modules.
fullDescription: Error E25 indicates an internal communication failure between the main control board and one of its sub-systems.

causes.cause: Loose ribbon cable to display board

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes to allow full board discharge and reset.

fixSteps.title: Use surge protection
fixSteps.instruction: Plug into a surge protector to prevent future board damage.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: If accessible, check the ribbon cable and connectors between the control and display boards.

fixSteps.title: Professional board repair
fixSteps.instruction: If E25 persists, the control or display board needs professional diagnosis and replacement.

videoSearchQuery: Liebherr refrigerator E25 control board communication error fix

faq.a: Control board replacement can be $200-$450 including parts and labor.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E25?
faq.a: Yes, power surges are a common cause of control board failures.

faq.q: Should I replace the board myself?
faq.a: Unless you have experience with appliance electronics, professional service is recommended.
